summary.text: Animation where the screens slightly shift to left/right
Route object for the current screen.
Event which fires on long press on the tab in the tab bar.
Event which fires on tapping on the tab in the tab bar.
Event which fires when a transition animation ends.
Event which fires when a transition animation starts.

How the screen should animate when switching tabs.

Supported values:
- 'none': don't animate the screen (default)
- 'fade': cross-fade the screens.
- 'shift': shift the screens slightly shift to left/right.
Whether inactive screens should be suspended from re-rendering. Defaults to

is run at the top of the application.
Requires

version >=3.16.0.

Only supported on iOS and Android.
Function that given returns a React Element to display as a header.
Whether to show the header. Setting this to
Whether this screens should render the first time it's accessed. Defaults to
if you want to render the screen on initial render.

Whether any nested stack should be popped to top when navigating away from the tab.
Defaults to
Style object for the component wrapping the screen content.
Function which specifies interpolated styles for bottom-tab scenes.

Accessibility label for the tab button. This is read by the screen reader when the user taps the tab.
It's recommended to set this if you don't have a label for the tab.
Background color for the active tab.
Color for the icon and label in the active tab.
Whether label font should scale to respect Text Size accessibility settings.

Function which returns a React Element to use as background for the tab bar.
You could render an image, a gradient, blur view etc.

When using

as well.
You'd also need to use
to add a bottom padding to your content.
Text to show in a badge on the tab icon.

Custom style for the tab bar badge.
You can specify a background color or text color here.

Function which returns a React element to render as the tab bar button.
Renders
ID to locate this tab button in tests.
Whether the tab bar gets hidden when the keyboard is shown. Defaults to
A function that given { focused: boolean, color: ColorValue } returns a React.Node to display in the tab bar.
Style object for the tab icon.
Background color for the inactive tabs.
Color for the icon and label in the inactive tabs.
Style object for the tab item container.

Title string of a tab displayed in the tab bar
or a function that given { focused: boolean, color: ColorValue, position: 'below-icon' | 'beside-icon', children: string } returns a React.Node to display in tab bar.

When undefined, scene title is used. Use

Whether the label is shown below the icon or beside the icon.

-

: the label is shown below the icon (typical for iPhones)
-

the label is shown next to the icon (typical for iPad)

By default, the position is chosen automatically based on device width.
Style object for the tab label.
Position of the tab bar on the screen. Defaults to
Whether the tab label should be visible. Defaults to
Style object for the tab bar container.
Variant of the tab bar. Defaults to
Animation config for showing and hiding the tab bar when the keyboard is shown/hidden.
Object which specifies the animation type (timing or spring) and their options (such as duration for timing).
